trigger_cases.prompt: Objdiff says this decomp unit mismatches; classify compiler versus source-shape causes.
Audit our objdiff target/base provenance before accepting a match.
The project report says 94% matched but hides missing units.
Configure the existing project objdiff workflow for one newly split function.
A stale base object produced an apparent clean comparison.
Interpret relocation-only differences in an object-level match.
The matcher reports BUILD_FAIL; should this unit count as mismatched?
Compare compiler flags using repeatable objdiff results across representative units.
Debug a runtime crash in the native executable.
Review two C source files for style differences.
Set up a project that uses a different authoritative binary matcher.
Produce a semantic equivalence proof without binary matching requirements.

output_cases.prompt: One tiny function matches, so the compiler and entire project are proven correct.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Limits the result to the identified unit/configuration and requires representative plus complete required-surface coverage and separate link/runtime gates.

output_cases.prompt: Count a BUILD_FAIL as a mismatch and include it in the match percentage denominator silently.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Reports build failure separately, exposes numerator/denominator and missing/failed/inconclusive units, and makes no comparison claim.

output_cases.prompt: The comparison is clean, but the target object provenance is unknown.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Marks the result inconclusive until exact original build/unit/section/relocation provenance and fresh base identity are established.
